explicit_text: Back in the day, before the Congress of Industrial Organizations, or CIO, came in, things were tough for Negro workers like me. White butchers didn't want us learning the trade and would throw anything at us to keep us out. But once the CIO got involved, it was like a bad dream was gone. The white men and Negro workers stood together, and the work environment improved, even though the work remained hard.
implicit_text: Back in the day, before the major federation of industrial unions came in, things were tough for Negro workers like me. White butchers didn't want us learning the trade and would throw anything at us to keep us out. But once that federation got involved, it was like a bad dream was gone. The white men and Negro workers stood together, and the work environment improved, even though the work remained hard.

explicit_text: I worked at Armour and Company for many years, starting on the killing floor and moving to the sheep kill where I learned butchering. In the 1930s, especially around 1933, I noticed they hired young white boys but rarely gave Negroes a chance to get those cleaner, easier jobs. I was even fired once by a drunk boss but was called back after three days without losing my seniority. Armour had a union that could be tough on Negro workers, but with the help of friends like Charlie, who vouched for me, I was able to get loans and stand my ground as a union brother.
implicit_text: I worked at a major meatpacking company for many years, starting on the killing floor and moving to the sheep kill where I learned butchering. In the 1930s, especially around 1933, I noticed they hired young white boys but rarely gave Negroes a chance to get those cleaner, easier jobs. I was even fired once by a drunk boss but was called back after three days without losing my seniority. The company had a union that could be tough on Negro workers, but with the help of friends like Charlie, who vouched for me, I was able to get loans and stand my ground as a union brother.
cues: killing floor and sheep kill jobs
